files_to_modify.change: Add path: str param to SegmentWiringClient.put_segment Protocol method; execute_segment computes path_arg (new_topic_path for CREATE_NEW, target_topic for APPEND_EXISTING) and passes it; GrpcPutSegmentClient.put_segment accepts path and passes to PutSegmentRequest(path=path); GrpcSegmentWiringClient.put_segment delegates path through. Update stale 'new_topic_path cannot be registered anywhere queryable today' module-docstring disclosure (now resolved).

_FakeWiringClient.put_segment gains path param, records 3-tuples; update 5 put_segment_calls assertions; update GrpcPutSegmentClient direct test to pass path and assert PutSegmentRequest called with path=; update GrpcSegmentWiringClient tests to pass path; add new test asserting path is populated correctly from PutSegmentRequest for both CREATE_NEW and APPEND_EXISTING via execute_segment through GrpcSegmentWiringClient (mocked stub).
_FakeWiringClient.put_segment gains path param, records 3-tuples; update 1 put_segment_calls assertion.

Go stub already regenerated in commit 1/3
Python stub already regenerated in commit 1/3; PutSegmentRequest already accepts path= kwarg
PathHash compute + pathIndex.Insert already landed in commit 2/3
does not document put_segment at Python-parameter granularity; no LLD-level change required for this internal Protocol signature change

downstream_consumers.name: agents/ingestion/execute_segment callers
downstream_consumers.note: grep confirms no other in-repo module calls execute_segment or SegmentWiringClient.put_segment besides the two test files listed above

risk: low — confined to one module + its two test files; no proto/Go/wire changes; signature change is additive at the wire level (path already existed on PutSegmentRequest) and internal-only at the Python Protocol level (no external consumers of SegmentWiringClient found)
